title: Commentary on Key Financial Performance – Q1'26
content:
The credit union financial position continues to be supported by exceptionally strong capital levels. The credit union reports a net worth ratio of 13.64%, representing an increase of 33.58 bps from the prior period, and a solvency ratio of 70%. This robust buffer provides ample room for absorbing potential losses while maintaining regulatory standing.

Asset quality remains stable and manageable. The delinquency ratio stands at 1.11% of total loans, while net charge-offs stand at 0.83% of average loans. Delinquency has improved by 66.43 bps from the prior period, indicating a strengthening in loan performance that supports continued monitoring.

Profitability remains positive. The credit union reports a return on average assets (ROAA) of 0.59% and a net interest margin of 3.81%, which increased by 6.14 bps from the prior period, reflecting improved earning-asset performance that supports overall profitability. Net operating expenses to average assets decreased by 37.52 bps, further supporting margins.

Balance-sheet structure continues to reflect a conservative operating profile. Loans represent 71.67% of shares and have declined by 98.36%, while shares and deposits have increased by 5.60%, reflecting continued balance-sheet contraction driven by membership attrition. The core funding ratio stands at 43.95%, demonstrating a reliance on external wholesale sources to fund loan growth.

Overall, the credit union enters Q1'26 with strong capitalization but faces headwinds in liquidity and profitability due to shrinking deposits. Membership retention will be critical for sustaining performance.
